div.head: The Ambassador in Argentina (Armour) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Buenos Aires, June 11, 1940—8 p.m.
[Received June 11—6:57 p.m.]

div.p:
206. Associated Press has just received substance of Vargas’ speech today and interprets a passage as possible endorsement of totalitarian
form of government as differentiated from the ideals enunciated by President Roosevelt last night.Address by President Roosevelt at Charlottesville, Virginia, June 10, 1940, Department of State Bulletin, June 15, 1940, p. 635. I should appreciate receiving for our confidential background here any comments.
